description: United National Dairy (KSA) inventory diagnostic. Shelf-life-aware across the dairy network: surfaces expiring batches, projected shelf-life shortfalls, and excess pile-up; recommends markdown / redirect / expedite / pause-inbound at batch grain.
tooltip_text: Focus: shelf-life-aware projected inventory + expiry-risk resolution for dairy and chilled SKUs.

solve.label: 🔭 Network — scan all
solve.text: Sweep my dairy network for the highest-value problem right now — stockout, expiry, or excess — tell me which SKU at which node it is, why it's happening, and the value-optimal action plan.

solve.label: SKU — YOGURT-FRUIT-4P
solve.text: YOGURT-FRUIT-4P is in sustained excess across the network — give me a value-optimal plan combining redirect to high-velocity stores, accelerated markdown on the oldest batches, and a hold on the next inbound orders.

solve.label: SKU × Route — LABAN on ROUTE-DAIRY-03 to STORE-EAST
solve.text: LABAN-500ML on ROUTE-DAIRY-03 to STORE-EAST has a projected stockout before the next delivery and one batch already at RSL=1 day — give me the action plan that resolves the gap and avoids the spoilage.

diagnose.label: SKU — MILK-FULL-1L
diagnose.text: Diagnose MILK-FULL-1L across the dairy network: which nodes have the highest combined stockout-and-expiry risk in the next 48 hours, and what's the underlying root cause for each?
